2606.28149.title: Toward Robust In-Context Segmentation via Concept Guidance
2606.28149.paper_summary: In-context segmentation (ICS) requires a model to segment target regions in a query image using only a few reference images and their corresponding masks, without updating any parameters. Despite recent progress, prior ICS studies have largely overlooked a critical aspect: system robustness, ie, whether the model can produce stable segmentation results for the same query under different references. In this work, we revisit ICS from the robustness perspective and introduce a novel paradigm, Concept-Guided In-Context Segmentation (CG-ICS), which performs segmentation by extracting high-level semantic concepts from references rather than relying solely on low-level visual matching. Specifically, CG-ICS introduces a concept reasoning module that uses an MLLM to propose candidates and a SAM3-driven scoring function with tree-search refinement to select reliable textual concepts, together with a parallel visual exemplar route that provides query-side spatial grounding via a simple context construction. Both the textual concept and the visual exemplar are then used to activate the segmentation capability of a frozen SAM3 backbone. Extensive experiments on standard ICS benchmarks demonstrate that CG-ICS not only achieves state-of-the-art accuracy but also substantially improves robustness, yielding a more reliable ICS system with significantly reduced variance across diverse reference choices.
2606.28149.paper_summary_zh: 在上下文分割（ICS）中，模型需要僅使用少量參考圖像及其相應的掩膜來分割查詢圖像中的目標區域，而無需更新任何參數。儘管最近取得了一些進展，但以往的ICS研究在很大程度上忽視了一個關鍵方面：系統穩健性，即模型是否能在不同參考下為相同查詢產生穩定的分割結果。在本研究中，我們從穩健性的角度重新審視ICS，並引入了一種新範式，即概念引導的上下文分割（CG-ICS），該方法通過從參考中提取高層次語義概念來進行分割，而不僅僅依賴低層次的視覺匹配。具體而言，CG-ICS引入了一個概念推理模塊，該模塊使用MLLM來提出候選項，並使用基於SAM3的評分函數和樹搜索優化來選擇可靠的文本概念，同時還有一條並行的視覺範例路徑，通過簡單的上下文構建為查詢側提供空間基礎。然後，文本概念和視覺範例一起用來激活凍結的SAM3主幹的分割能力。在標準ICS基準上的大量實驗表明，CG-ICS不僅達到了最先進的準確性，還顯著提高了穩健性，從而產生了一個更可靠的ICS系統，並在多樣的參考選擇中顯著減少了方差。
2606.28149.author: Zhigang Chen et.al.
2606.28149.authors: Zhigang Chen, Xiawu Zheng, Rongrong Ji
